title: Fix build error with GCC by forcing Clang in CMake on android/aarch64
body:
GCC does not recognize Clang-specific warning flags like -Wunreachable-code-break and -Wunreachable-code-return, which are passed by upstream submodules (e.g., ggml). This patch forces CMake to use Clang via command-line arguments, avoiding the need to patch nested submodules.

This resolves compiler errors without modifying submodule source code.

step_plan.summary: Ensure Android/aarch64 builds never use GCC by adding CMake gating that requires Clang at configure time and by tightening existing compiler validation in src/ to reject GNU on that target, avoiding failures from upstream Clang-only warning flags without patching submodules.

steps.operation: Add early Android/aarch64 compiler enforcement (require Clang or fail with actionable instructions) before subdirectories are configured
steps.reason: CMake compiler selection must be finalized at (or before) the top-level configuration so that all subprojects/submodules (e.g., llama.cpp/ggml) inherit a Clang toolchain; otherwise GCC will choke on upstream Clang-only warning flags like -Wunreachable-code-break/-return.
steps.side_effects: Android/aarch64 configurations that currently pick up GCC will now fail fast (or be forced) during configure with a clear message; may require users/CI to pass -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER=clang -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=clang++ (or NDK clang paths). Non-Android targets should remain unaffected if gated correctly. Re-configure required after changing compilers.

steps.operation: Tighten compiler sanity checks to disallow GNU specifically on Android/aarch64 (keep existing allowances elsewhere)
steps.reason: The src-level check currently allows GCC or Clang, but on Android/aarch64 GCC is not compatible with Clang-specific warning flags coming from upstream submodules; aligning this check with the top-level enforcement prevents late-stage build failures and produces a clearer diagnostic.
steps.side_effects: Any build that reaches src/ configuration on Android/aarch64 with GNU will now hard-fail earlier with an explanatory error. May affect downstream consumers that include this project as a subdirectory if they attempt to use GCC for Android/aarch64; documentation/CI may need updates to ensure Clang is selected.
